Introduction.Name: Daniele Primasso
Introduction.Summary: Experienced Lead Software Developer with a strong background in backend development, smart contract development, and blockchain technologies. Proven track record in designing and implementing API services, developing decentralized applications, and leading development teams. Adept in various programming languages including JavaScript, Go, Python, and Solidity.

JavaScript.Description: Interpreted scripting language, primarily used for web development.
JavaScript.Advantages: Large developer community, dynamic and versatile.
JavaScript.Disadvantages: Weak typing, potential for hard-to-detect bugs.

TypeScript.Description: Superset of JavaScript that adds static typing.
TypeScript.Usage: Used to write more robust and maintainable code.
TypeScript.Advantages: Reduces runtime errors, improved autocompletion and refactoring.
TypeScript.Disadvantages: Requires initial setup, learning curve for those unfamiliar with static typing.

Go.Description: Statically typed programming language developed by Google.
Go.Usage: Used to develop the core of the backend application.
Go.Advantages: Native concurrency, fast compilation, garbage collection, high performance.
Go.Disadvantages: Lack of generics (recently introduced), learning curve for those coming from dynamic languages.

Solidity.Description: Contract-oriented programming language for writing smart contracts.
Solidity.Usage: Used for developing smart contracts on Ethereum and other blockchain platforms.
Solidity.Advantages: Specifically designed for blockchain development, strong community support.
Solidity.Disadvantages: Young language with evolving best practices, security vulnerabilities if not used correctly.

Node.js.Description: JavaScript runtime built on Chrome's V8 engine.
Node.js.Usage: Used as the execution environment for the backend server.
Node.js.Advantages: High performance due to event-driven architecture, uses JavaScript on both server and client sides.
Node.js.Disadvantages: Complex handling of CPU-intensive operations, callback hell without async/await.

Go Runtime.Description: Runtime environment for programs written in Go.
Go Runtime.Usage: Used as the execution environment for the backend.
Go Runtime.Advantages: Optimized for high performance, native support for concurrency.
Go Runtime.Disadvantages: Lower level compared to runtimes like Node.js, requires manual management of goroutines.

Express.js.Usage: Used to handle routes and middleware for the backend server.
Express.js.Advantages: Ease of use, wide range of available middleware.
Express.js.Disadvantages: Less structured compared to frameworks like Rails or Django.

Ethers.js.Usage: Used to interface with smart contracts and Ethereum nodes.
Ethers.js.Advantages: Simple and intuitive API, support for various providers.
Ethers.js.Disadvantages: Depends on the stability of network connections.

Gin.Usage: Used to handle routes and middleware for the backend server.
Gin.Advantages: High performance, simple and intuitive API, middleware support.
Gin.Disadvantages: Less feature-rich compared to heavier frameworks like Django.

Gorm.Usage: Used to interact with the database in an ORM fashion.
Gorm.Advantages: Ease of use, support for multiple databases, automatic migrations.
Gorm.Disadvantages: Lower performance compared to hand-written SQL queries.

Apollo Client.Description: Library for managing GraphQL queries on the client side.
Apollo Client.Usage: Used to make GraphQL queries and manage application state.
Apollo Client.Disadvantages: Additional complexity compared to REST, requires good understanding of GraphQL.

Axios.Description: A Promise-based HTTP client for the browser and Node.js.
Axios.Disadvantages: Slightly larger footprint compared to native fetch.

Handlebars.Disadvantages: Limited in logic complexity compared to JSX or other templating engines.

TSLint.Usage: Enforces code style and best practices.
TSLint.Advantages: Consistent code style, catches potential errors.

dotenv.Description: A module for loading environment variables from a .env file.

MongoDB Go Driver.Usage: Used for database operations such as querying and updating MongoDB collections.
MongoDB Go Driver.Advantages: Strong typing, integration with Go's context package for managing deadlines and cancellations.
MongoDB Go Driver.Disadvantages: More verbose than other MongoDB clients, requires understanding of Go concurrency model.

Chi.Description: Lightweight, idiomatic and composable router for building Go HTTP services.
Chi.Usage: Used to manage routing in the Go web server.
Chi.Disadvantages: Less feature-rich compared to heavier frameworks like Gin or Echo.

Mongoose.Description: An Object Data Modeling (ODM) library for MongoDB and Node.js.
Mongoose.Usage: Used to model application data in MongoDB, providing schema-based data validation and querying.
Mongoose.Advantages: Schema validation, middleware support, easy data manipulation.
Mongoose.Disadvantages: Additional abstraction layer can lead to performance overhead, learning curve for Mongoose-specific methods.

Hardhat.Description: Development environment for compiling, deploying, testing, and debugging Ethereum software.
Hardhat.Usage: Used to manage the Ethereum smart contract development workflow.
Hardhat.Advantages: Extensible, integrates with various plugins, supports TypeScript.
Hardhat.Disadvantages: Steeper learning curve compared to simpler tools like Truffle.

Jest.Usage: Used for writing and running unit tests for the project.
Jest.Advantages: Easy to set up, great documentation, snapshot testing.
Jest.Disadvantages: Can be slow for large test suites, potential configuration overhead.

React.Description: JavaScript library for building user interfaces.
React.Disadvantages: Requires learning JSX, complexity in large applications.

Redux.Description: State management library for JavaScript applications.
Redux.Usage: Used to manage the state of the application.
Redux.Advantages: Predictable state management, central state store.

OpenAI.Usage: Provides various AI models and APIs for different use cases.
OpenAI.Advantages: Cutting-edge AI technology, wide range of applications.
OpenAI.Disadvantages: Can be costly, requires understanding of AI principles.

MongoDB.Usage: Used as the main database for the application.
MongoDB.Disadvantages: Eventual consistency (instead of strong consistency), complexity in handling complex queries.

PostgreSQL.Usage: Used as the main database for the application.
PostgreSQL.Advantages: ACID compliant, supports complex operations and transactions, extensibility.
PostgreSQL.Disadvantages: Complex initial configuration, lower performance compared to NoSQL for some operations.

JSON-RPC.Description: Lightweight protocol for remote procedure calls using JSON.
JSON-RPC.Usage: Used to communicate with Ethereum nodes.
JSON-RPC.Disadvantages: Limited compared to other protocols like gRPC.

GraphQL.Description: Query language for APIs developed by Facebook.
GraphQL.Usage: Used for API queries and mutations on the client side.
GraphQL.Advantages: Requests only the necessary data, reduces over-fetching, powerful typing.
GraphQL.Disadvantages: Initial complexity, requires a GraphQL server.

REST API.Description: Representational state transfer is a software architectural style that defines a set of constraints to be used for creating Web services.
REST API.Usage: Used to create endpoints for the backend service.
REST API.Disadvantages: Can lead to multiple round trips to the server for complex queries.

Swagger.Description: Tool for designing, building, documenting, and using RESTful APIs.
Swagger.Advantages: Facilitates understanding and usage of APIs, automatically generates documentation.

Middleware Pattern.Description: A pattern for handling requests and responses in a modular way.
Middleware Pattern.Usage: Used in src/middlewares/ for auth and email handling.
Middleware Pattern.Disadvantages: Can become hard to manage with many middlewares.

Controller Pattern.Description: A pattern for separating business logic from routing.
Controller Pattern.Usage: Used in src/controllers/ for handling specific functionalities like error handling and email.
Controller Pattern.Advantages: Clear separation of concerns, easier testing.
Controller Pattern.Disadvantages: Can become bloated if not managed properly.

Repository Pattern.Description: Mediates between the domain and data mapping layers using a collection-like interface for accessing domain objects.
Repository Pattern.Usage: Used in `internal/db/mongo.go` to abstract the database operations.
Repository Pattern.Advantages: Decouples business logic from data access, easier testing.
Repository Pattern.Disadvantages: Can introduce additional complexity, potential performance overhead.

Singleton Pattern.Description: Ensures a class has only one instance and provides a global point of access to it.
Singleton Pattern.Usage: Used in the database connection setup to ensure only one connection pool is created.
Singleton Pattern.Advantages: Controlled access to the sole instance, reduced memory footprint.
Singleton Pattern.Disadvantages: Can be difficult to implement in a thread-safe way, global state can make testing harder.

Observer Pattern.Description: Defines a one-to-many dependency between objects.
Observer Pattern.Usage: Used to create a subscription mechanism.
Observer Pattern.Advantages: Promotes loose coupling, easy to extend.

ERC Standards: ERC20: Standard interface for fungible tokens
ERC721: Standard interface for non-fungible tokens (NFTs)
ERC1155: Standard interface for multi-token types (both fungible and non-fungible)
ERC1271: Standard interface for validating signatures on behalf of a contract
Protocols: VotingEscrow: Protocol for time-locked staking and governance
Development Tools: Ganache: Personal blockchain for Ethereum development
Remix: Web-based IDE for Solidity development
Projects: Curve: DeFi protocol for stablecoin trading
Compound: DeFi protocol for lending and borrowing
Zapper: DeFi dashboard for managing assets

Responsibilities: Design and implementation of API services for microservices
Primary languages used: Node.js and Go
Solidity development using standards such as ERC20, ERC721, ERC1155, ERC1271, ERC4337
Research and development in emerging projects such as play-to-earn, NFT tokenization, and trading
Maintenance and development of travel agency software for Expedia using Java (Grails framework)
Participation in the Tech Companies Lab 2019 project, awarded special mention for complex cloud, big data, and IoT projects
Maintenance and migration of management software for HP
Development of software for machine automation using PLC
Development of IoT project prototype for waste disposal machine
Development of machine prototype for digitizing the cataloging process of mechanical parts

Machine Learning.Description: Field of study that gives computers the ability to learn without being explicitly programmed.
Machine Learning.Usage: Used to build models that can make predictions or decisions without human intervention.
Machine Learning.Advantages: Automates analytical model building, can process large amounts of data.
Machine Learning.Disadvantages: Requires large datasets, can be computationally intensive.

AWS.Description: Amazon Web Services, a comprehensive cloud computing platform.
AWS.Usage: Used for cloud storage, computing power, and various cloud services.
AWS.Advantages: Scalability, reliability, wide range of services.
AWS.Disadvantages: Complex pricing model, steep learning curve.

Python.Description: High-level programming language known for its readability and versatility.
Python.Usage: Used in web development, data analysis, artificial intelligence, and more.
Python.Advantages: Easy to read and write, large standard library, strong community support.
Python.Disadvantages: Slower than compiled languages, can have runtime errors due to dynamic typing.

Ton Ecosystem.Description: Ecosystem around the Telegram Open Network, a blockchain-based platform.
Ton Ecosystem.Usage: Used for building decentralized applications and smart contracts.
Ton Ecosystem.Advantages: High transaction speed, strong community support.

Near Ecosystem.Description: Ecosystem around the NEAR Protocol, a blockchain-based platform.
Near Ecosystem.Usage: Used for building decentralized applications and smart contracts.
